title: Add embedding cache to reduce duplicate computation in dedup
description: memory/embeddings.py computes embeddings for every finding on every run, even if the text was embedded yesterday. A simple disk cache (sqlite or pickle) keyed by text hash would eliminate redundant embedding calls and speed up the dedup phase.
requirements: Add a cache layer to the embedding function
Cache key: SHA256 of input text
Cache store: SQLite table in memory.db or separate cache.db
Cache hit skips model inference entirely
Cache invalidation: none needed (embeddings are deterministic for same model)
done_criteria: Second run with same findings is >50% faster in embedding phase
Cache hits logged at INFO level
context: bge-small-en-v1.5 is fast but computing 400 embeddings per run is still ~30s. Cache would cut this to <1s on repeat items.
